capability_gaps.current_state: No commitment devices, loss aversion triggers, variable reward schedules, or social proof mechanics. Users abandon at 74-77% on Day-1 with no psychological scaffolding to re-engage.
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Integrated behavioral economics engine with habit loops, variable reward scheduling, micro-commitment sequences, and loss framing applied at key dropout moments — especially the Day-1 through Day-7 window.
risks: Dark pattern accusations if loss aversion mechanics are perceived as manipulative
Over-engineering retention mechanics before validating core product value
A/B testing infrastructure required to measure which interventions work — adds engineering overhead
capability_gaps.cross_portfolio_synergy: Retention frameworks are reusable across any consumer subscription product targeting habit formation — edtech, wellness, productivity apps.

capability_gaps.current_state: No persistent agent memory — AI context resets between sessions, making each coaching interaction stateless. Personalization cannot compound over time, undermining the core AI value proposition.
capability_gaps.ideal_state: A lightweight user memory layer (vector store or structured profile) that persists goals, progress, preferences, injury flags, and motivational patterns across sessions — enabling coaching continuity and compounding personalization within the $200/month AI budget constraint.
risks: Vector store and embedding costs could breach $200/month AI budget at scale
Privacy and GDPR compliance risk from storing rich behavioral user profiles
Cold-start problem remains until sufficient interaction history is accumulated
Memory retrieval latency could degrade real-time coaching UX
capability_gaps.cross_portfolio_synergy: Persistent memory architecture pattern is reusable for any AI coaching or tutoring product requiring longitudinal user context.

capability_gaps.current_state: 74-77% Day-1 abandonment signals a catastrophic onboarding failure. Users likely face friction, unclear value demonstration, or mismatched expectation-setting before experiencing the AI coaching differentiation.
capability_gaps.ideal_state: A frictionless, emotionally resonant onboarding flow that delivers a personalized 'wow moment' within the first 3 minutes — demonstrating AI adaptability before asking for commitment, using progressive disclosure to minimize cognitive overload.
risks: Solo founder has no dedicated design resource — risk of UX improvements being deprioritized under engineering load
Without user research, redesigned onboarding may solve the wrong friction points
A/B testing onboarding variants requires instrumentation that adds sprint overhead
capability_gaps.cross_portfolio_synergy: Onboarding conversion frameworks applicable to any freemium consumer app with a complex AI-driven core product.

capability_gaps.current_state: Product appears functionally driven — workout generation and plan adaptation without an emotional story arc or identity-based motivation system. Users are treated as data inputs rather than protagonists in a transformation journey.
capability_gaps.ideal_state: A narrative layer that frames the user's fitness journey as a personal story with identity transformation milestones, emotionally resonant language, and coaching voice that shifts based on user psychological profile — converting functional utility into emotional loyalty.
risks: Narrative tone must be calibrated per user segment — a tone that motivates one user may alienate another
Over-narrativizing a functional fitness tool risks feeling gimmicky to data-driven users
Requires tight coordination with Marcus (UX) and Flow (psychology) to avoid fragmented experience
capability_gaps.cross_portfolio_synergy: Emotional narrative design frameworks transfer directly to mental health, recovery, and chronic disease management apps.

capability_gaps.current_state: No automated or data-driven mechanism to identify high-impact product improvements or predict which users are at risk of churning. Feature prioritization is manual and founder-intuition driven.
capability_gaps.ideal_state: A lightweight behavioral analytics and early churn signal system that tracks leading indicators of disengagement (session frequency drops, skipped workouts, reduced interaction depth) and surfaces actionable retention triggers — operable within Supabase/PostgreSQL infrastructure.
risks: Insufficient user volume at MVP stage may produce statistically unreliable churn signals
Building analytics infrastructure competes with core product development for solo founder bandwidth
Risk of over-indexing on lagging metrics (churn) rather than leading product quality signals
capability_gaps.cross_portfolio_synergy: Churn prediction models and behavioral telemetry pipelines are directly reusable across any subscription consumer app.

capability_gaps.current_state: AI-generated workout plans may lack grounding in validated exercise science principles — progressive overload, periodization, recovery windows, and injury prevention — risking user harm, poor results, and credibility damage.
capability_gaps.ideal_state: An exercise science knowledge layer embedded into the AI coaching system — validating generated workout plans against biomechanical principles, recovery requirements, and progressive overload models before delivery to users, with flagging for contraindicated movement patterns.
risks: Legal liability if AI-generated plans cause injury without proper safety guardrails
Exercise science constraints may conflict with AI personalization flexibility — requires careful system prompt engineering
Scope creep risk if exercise science layer becomes overly complex for MVP stage
capability_gaps.cross_portfolio_synergy: Exercise science validation logic and injury flag systems are reusable across rehabilitation, physical therapy, and senior fitness products.

capability_gaps.current_state: No evidence of privacy framework, health data handling policies, or compliance architecture. Storing behavioral profiles, workout performance data, and psychological motivation patterns creates significant regulatory exposure — especially under GDPR, CCPA, and potential HIPAA adjacency.
capability_gaps.ideal_state: A lightweight compliance foundation including privacy policy, health data handling disclosures, consent flows for AI personalization data, and data minimization practices — sufficient to protect against regulatory risk and build user trust without enterprise compliance overhead.
risks: Health-adjacent data (recovery patterns, psychological profiles) may trigger HIPAA considerations in some jurisdictions
Non-compliance discovered post-scale creates costly retroactive remediation
Over-engineering compliance at MVP stage wastes constrained founder bandwidth
capability_gaps.cross_portfolio_synergy: Privacy and health data compliance frameworks are foundational across all health, wellness, and fitness products in the portfolio.

capability_gaps.current_state: Manual, founder-intuition-driven feature prioritization with no structured framework for evaluating retention impact versus development cost — creating risk of building low-leverage features while critical retention gaps remain unaddressed.
capability_gaps.ideal_state: A lean product management system with an ICE or RICE scoring framework, a retention-weighted prioritization model, and a structured sprint cadence — enabling the solo founder to make defensible, data-informed build decisions under bandwidth constraints.
risks: Framework adoption overhead may feel bureaucratic for a solo founder in rapid iteration mode
Prioritization system is only as good as the behavioral data feeding it — requires Pulse's churn signals to be meaningful
Risk of analysis paralysis if prioritization process becomes too formalized
capability_gaps.cross_portfolio_synergy: Retention-weighted prioritization frameworks applicable to any early-stage consumer product with constrained engineering resources.
